questions.question: Why did the British introduce the Permanent Settlement in Bengal?
questions.answer: The British introduced the Permanent Settlement to ensure a fixed and regular income from land revenue.

questions.question: What role did zamindars play under the Permanent Settlement?
questions.answer: Zamindars were responsible for collecting land revenue from peasants and paying it to the British government.

questions.question: Why did many zamindars lose their land during British rule?
questions.answer: Many zamindars lost their land because they could not pay the fixed land revenue on time.

questions.question: What happened to zamindari lands that were not able to pay revenue?
questions.answer: The lands were auctioned by the British government.

questions.question: Why did peasants fall into debt during colonial rule?
questions.answer: Peasants fell into debt because of heavy taxes and loans taken from moneylenders.

questions.question: Why did European planters want peasants to grow indigo?
questions.answer: European planters wanted peasants to grow indigo because it produced blue dye which was in high demand in Europe.

questions.question: What problem did peasants face when they accepted advances from planters?
questions.answer: Peasants were forced to grow indigo instead of food crops after accepting advances.

questions.question: Why did peasants prefer growing food crops rather than indigo?
questions.answer: Peasants preferred growing food crops because they needed food for survival.

questions.question: How did indigo cultivation affect the soil?
questions.answer: Indigo cultivation exhausted the soil and reduced its fertility.

questions.question: What was the main cause of the Indigo Revolt?
questions.answer: The main cause of the Indigo Revolt was the forced cultivation of indigo by European planters.

questions.question: How did peasants protest during the Indigo Revolt?
questions.answer: Peasants protested by refusing to grow indigo.

questions.question: What role did moneylenders play in rural society?
questions.answer: Moneylenders provided loans to peasants, often charging high interest.

questions.question: Why were peasants dependent on moneylenders?
questions.answer: Peasants depended on moneylenders to pay taxes and meet farming expenses.

questions.question: Which region became the centre of indigo cultivation?
questions.answer: Bengal became the centre of indigo cultivation.

questions.question: Why was indigo considered a cash crop?
questions.answer: Indigo was considered a cash crop because it was grown mainly for trade and profit.

questions.question: What did the British government do after the Indigo Revolt?
questions.answer: The British government set up the Indigo Commission to investigate the revolt.

questions.question: What was the purpose of the Indigo Commission?
questions.answer: The Indigo Commission was set up to investigate the problems faced by peasants in indigo cultivation.

questions.question: How did colonial policies affect Indian agriculture?
questions.answer: Colonial policies forced peasants to grow cash crops and increased their economic difficulties.

questions.question: Why were peasants unhappy with European planters?
questions.answer: Peasants were unhappy because planters forced them to grow indigo and paid very low prices.

questions.question: What was one effect of heavy land revenue demands?
questions.answer: Heavy land revenue demands pushed many peasants into poverty and debt.

questions.question: Who benefited most from indigo cultivation?
questions.answer: European planters benefited most from indigo cultivation.

questions.question: Why did indigo cultivation reduce food production?
questions.answer: Indigo cultivation used land that could otherwise be used for growing food crops.

questions.question: What does the Indigo Revolt reveal about peasant resistance?
questions.answer: It shows that peasants could unite and protest against exploitation.

questions.question: How did colonial land policies change rural society?
questions.answer: Colonial land policies created new landlords and increased economic pressure on peasants.

questions.question: Why is the Indigo Revolt important in Indian history?
questions.answer: The Indigo Revolt was important because it showed organized resistance by peasants against colonial exploitation.
